---
title: "Hantavirus Assay Design"
subtitle: "A molecular diagnostics case study on hantavirus phylogeny, epidemiology, and hypothetical TaqMan-style assay design."
date: "2026-05-22"
categories: [molecular diagnostics, virology, phylogenetics, qpcr]
image: cover.svg
image-alt: "Abstract molecular diagnostics graphic for a hantavirus assay-design project"
---

An educational portfolio notebook using the 2026 MV Hondius Andes virus cluster
as a case study for infectious-disease assay-development reasoning. The project
connects virology, epidemiology, phylogenetics, in-silico assay review, and
validation planning without providing deployable primer/probe sequences or bench
protocols.

## What's inside

- **Current-event framing** - public-health context for the MV Hondius Andes
  virus cluster.
- **Virology and epidemiology** - hantavirus taxonomy, genome structure, host
  ecology, and transmission considerations.
- **Assay-design workflow** - conceptual TaqMan-style RT-qPCR target-selection,
  inclusivity, exclusivity, and change-control criteria.
- **Validation plan** - analytical-risk questions that would need to be answered
  before any laboratory or clinical use.

## Links

- **[Rendered notebook](hantavirus_taqman_assay_design.html)** - full HTML case
  study.
- **[Notebook source](hantavirus_taqman_assay_design.qmd)** - Quarto source.
- **[Jupyter notebook](hantavirus_taqman_assay_design.ipynb)** - notebook export.
